title: Rhopalopodium ranunculinum
taxonomy: Rhopalopodium ranunculinum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. This family is commonly known as the buttercup or crowfoot family. Rhopalopodium ranunculinum is native to the Andean region of South America. It is a type of alpine plant that grows in high-altitude habitats. The plant is also known as the 'Buttercup Bearberry'.
uses: Rhopalopodium ranunculinum, also known as the alpine cat's ear, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. The plant's extracts have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help alleviate various health conditions. Some research suggests that Rhopalopodium ranunculinum may have antimicrobial properties, useful in fighting off certain types of infections. Further studies are needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications. The plant's traditional use in folk medicine has been documented in some regions, highlighting its potential as a valuable natural resource.

constituents: Rhopalopodium ranunculinum, also known as the snow lotus, is a plant native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas. side_effects: Rhopalopodium ranunculinum contains ranunculin, a glucosinolate that can be toxic if ingested. If ingested in large quantities, it may cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Contact with the plant's sap can also cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. Ingestion of ranunculin can also lead to more severe health issues, such as kidney damage and respiratory problems. It is essential to handle the plant with caution and avoid any contact if you are experiencing symptoms of an allergic reaction.
